mnemonics: CODE Blocks: Curly Braces Organize Detailed Execution
keyPoints: A code block is a group of statements enclosed in curly braces {}.
Blocks define a new scope, and variables declared inside are local to that block.
Blocks are used to group statements for control flow structures like if, for, while, and methods.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use curly braces {}, which can lead to logical errors (e.g., an 'if' statement only executing the next line).
Trying to use a variable outside of the block it was declared in, resulting in a compilation error.
